original_reasoning: Laban - white (milk) vs Lebanon - mountain named from 'white' (whiteness of snow/chalk), shared Semitic root
Arabic naming and Greek notes/markings share a clear signifying-recording core.
Firm establishment and rooted knowledge plausibly deepen into a doctrinal school leader.

العتق = freeing, emancipation (عتيق = freed; مولى عتاقة = freedman, emancipated slave). καταλυτικός = able to dissolve, able to end/undo. Both concepts center on the dissolution of bonds — العتق is specifically the loosening of the bond of slavery, καταλύω is loosening/dissolving in general. The semantic core of 'breaking/loosening a binding constraint' is directly shared.
A joint or tightly linked mail fits receiving/holding together more clearly.
Arabic صقلاب refers to Slav/red-haired strong-eating; Greek Σκλάβος is 'Slav'. Direct ethnonym match; Arabic preserves Greek/Byzantine ethnic term
Arabic denotes weak-mindedness despite large size, and λήθετε relates to forgetting/mental lapse; both share a core of impaired cognition.
Arabic peel or outer layer aligns reasonably with Greek shoe or sock as an external covering.
